title: Enterprise Asset Management
description: **Enterprise Asset Management** Join a leading industrial consulting practice that combines deep engineering expertise with cutting\-edge technologies to help organizations in Utilities, Health, and Public Sector transform operations and maximize asset value. This Enterprise Asset Management role offers the chance to deliver technology\-driven, value\-focused solutions across the full asset lifecycle. **About the Role:** In this Enterprise Asset Management role, you will help clients modernize operations, integrate digital technologies, and improve asset performance. This is a dynamic role that blends consulting, technology, and operational excellence, with opportunities to apply AI, IoT, and digital twins to real\-world challenges. The position is hybrid, based in either **Cork** or **Dublin** , with some travel expected. **Responsibilities:** * Deliver end\-to\-end asset management solutions, including strategy, process design, and capability uplift. * Facilitate client workshops, align stakeholders, and drive cross\-functional change. * Advise on enterprise technology selection and integration (EAM/ERP platforms like Maximo, SAP, IFS). * Apply AI, IoT, OT/IT integration, and digital technologies to modernize asset operations. * Support proposal development, client presentations, and value\-driven transformation engagements. **About You:** * Significant experience in asset management consulting across utilities, healthcare, or public infrastructure. * Strong technical expertise in Enterprise Asset Management systems, IoT, SCADA, and digital twins. * Experience delivering digital transformation projects with cloud\-based EAM platforms. * Familiarity with industry standards (ISO 55000, BIM/ISO19650\) and asset lifecycle frameworks. * Excellent consulting, communication, and stakeholder management skills.

title: European CISO
description: At FNZ, our purpose is to make wealth management more accessible, bringing easier, fairer and more inclusive solutions to people worldwide. Here in the Global Information Security team, we are on a mission to embed cyber resilience across FNZ, protecting the platforms that support investment solutions for over 20 million people. We are looking for an exceptional Regional Information Security Officer to develop and lead a local operational security team in the delivery of FNZ’s cyber resiliency strategy. You will report into the Head of Information Security and have significant experience in regulated financial services. You will have good knowledge of a variety of information security frameworks, legal and regulatory requirements, coupled with an ability to communicate risk effectively to stakeholders of all levels. You will have previous experience of complex, rapidly changing environments. You will be able to rapidly identify areas of improvement and make changes for the good of the business. You will work alongside the wider business to implement excellent cyber resiliency practices across FNZ. **Specific Role Responsibilities** * Lead in the management of information security across in scope businesses. * Manage business and external stakeholder (i.e. regulator, client) engagement. * Lead the local development, implementation, delivery and support of FNZ’s Cyber Resilience strategy in alignment with our organisational mission. * Support the Group CISO in the delivery of the Group Cyber Resilience strategy. * Contribute to the development and communication of corporate information security policy, standards and guidelines. * Lead the local provision of services to support all client and regulatory requirements such as audit preparation, support for RFPs and contractual client information security committees. * Lead a local team of Officers and/or Analysts to deliver a consistent global service, supporting the delivery of business goals with expert guidance. * Lead the way in implementing security culture across the business. * Lead the assessment, analysis and reporting of information and cyber security risks to the appropriate audiences. **Primary Requirements** * Significant experience leading an Information Security team / function in a Financial Services context. * Good knowledge of the practical implementation of information security and risk management frameworks such as ISO27001, NIST 800\-53 and COBIT. * Good knowledge of audit frameworks such as ISAE3402 and SOC2\. * Experienced in European regulations and legislation such as GDPR, DORA, AI ACT. * Highly experienced in managing client and regulator relationships. * Exceptional ability to manage internal stakeholders through a journey of improving information security maturity * Exceptional ability to communicate information security and risk concepts to stakeholders of all levels. * Hold professional Information Security qualifications such as CISSP, CISM, CISA, CRISC. **Secondary Requirements** * Experience in the implementation and management of ISO27001 certification. * Experience delivering compliance with PCI DSS and Swift standards. * Leading a small team of Information Security professionals **About FNZ Culture** Our culture is what drives us. It's at the heart of who we are and everything we do. It's what inspires, excites and moves us forward. Our ambition is to create a culture for growth, one that opens up limitless opportunities for our employees, customers and the wider world. At FNZ we know that great impact is only possible with great teamwork. That’s why we value the strength and diversity of thought in our global team. The FNZ Way is the cornerstone of what we do. It is comprised of four values that set the standard for how everyone at FNZ interacts with each other, with our customers, and with all our diverse stakeholders around the world. * Customer obsessed for the long\-term * Think big and make an impact * Act now and own it all the way * Challenge, commit and win together Read more about The FNZ Way and our values: www.fnz.com/culture **About FNZ** *FNZ is committed to opening up wealth so that everyone, everywhere can invest in their future on their terms. We know the foundation to do that already exists in the wealth management industry, but complexity holds firms back.* *We created wealth’s growth platform to help. We provide a global, end\-to\-end wealth management platform that integrates modern technology with business and investment operations. All in a regulated financial institution.* *We partner with the world’s leading financial institutions, with over US$2\.4 trillion in assets on platform (AoP). title: Executive Sous Chef - Anantara the Marker Dublin Hotel

From pristine islands and tropical beaches to cosmopolitan cities, tranquil deserts and lush jungles, Anantara welcomes guests for journeys rich in discovery.** **Anantara The Marker Dublin Hotel is a sleek, contemporary landmark inspired by the elements and overlooking the waterfront of Grand Canal Square. The area is home to tech and finance giants, chic restaurants and world\-class theatres.** Specific Duties * To support the Head Chef in day to day running of the kitchen * To report all the incident/accident reports as per company policy and to ensure that corrective action is taken * To ensure that rosters are complete and shared two weeks in advance * To manage absence of team * To assist with the Head Chef with the performance management of the Kitchen Team and to ensure that regular job chats/appraisals are taking place * To review the Kitchen Risk Assessment on bi\-annual basis and update the document when needed * To ensure that all Kitchen Team members are trained in Basic Full Hygiene and comply with the HACCP * To ensure that all purchases are processed via SAP * Self\-motivated and sets a positive example for employees by their attitude and performance * Demonstrates high levels of energy, enthusiasm and professionalism * Encourages the team towards Hotel and individual objectives and aims * Shows concern for their team members and interacts with them in a positive manner * Provides a great work environment and treating each other with dignity and respect and embracing diversity * Demonstrates strong leadership skills and regularly shows an ability to adjust their approach to deal with different people and situations * Communicates in a structured and effective manner with their team * Builds and sustains effective relationships with employees and customers * Motivates, inspires and empowers others to improve performance Must currently have the right to work Full time in Ireland Knowledge of Thai culture valued The position starts in September 2026 **Are you looking for a new challenge?** **Apply now!** *Minor Hotels Europe \& Americas is dedicated to fostering an inclusive working environment where every individual is valued and equally encouraged. We warmly welcome individuals from all backgrounds and abilities.*

title: Executive Valuer
description: **The Job** Reporting to a Senior Executive Valuer, or such designated Officer as may be determined from time to time, the **Executive Valuer** will work as part of a multi\-disciplinary team within the City Valuer’s Office and the wider Planning and Property Development Department. They will provide reports and advice to the team, deliver a programme of valuation work and will provide professional advice to the City Council on all valuation and associated matters. This is a real and exciting opportunity to contribute to shaping the Dublin City of the future. The work is very varied, interesting and provides opportunities to achieve high levels of professional development. Within the structure of the City Valuers Office there will be amble scope to compete for promotion to higher grades over time. Higher grades include Senior Executive Valuer and Senior Valuer. **The ideal candidate shall** * possess strong professional knowledge and skills, including well\-honed negotiating skills; * have a good working knowledge or demonstrate an ability to acquire a good working knowledge of the legal, regulatory and governance framework within which the City Council operates and adhere to corporate policies, protocols and procedures; * demonstrate a good working knowledge of relevant legislation and case law; * be capable of producing high quality valuations and reports, in high volume; * possess satisfactory experience supervising staff and be able to work within and lead a multi\-disciplined team; * have the ability to motivate and encourage personnel under their control to achieve maximum performance; * have an ability to work independently and on their own initiative to progress diverse casework in a professional manner; * have strong communication, organisation, I.T. and presentation skills; * be willing to learn new skills in the areas of property valuation and development, and be interested in, and prepared to learn about, the activities of Dublin City Council; * be willing to undertake continuing professional development and, as required, to work towards becoming a Chartered Surveyor; * have good knowledge and awareness of Health \& Safety Legislation and Regulations, their role and duties of managers in safety management and their application in the workplace; * have a satisfactory knowledge of public administration in Ireland and experience of administration. ***On the date of appointment the successful candidate must possess a current unendorsed full driving licence (Category A1, A and/or B) as they may be required to drive in the course of their duties.*** **Qualifications** **Character:** Each candidate shall be of good character. **Health:** Candidates shall be in a state of health such as would indicate a reasonable prospect of ability to render regular and efficient service. **Education, Experience, etc.:** Each candidate must, on the latest date for receipt of completed application forms: \- **(a) (i)** have passed, or be exempt by examination from, the Final Examination of the Society of Chartered Surveyors Ireland/Royal Institution of Chartered Surveyors (Valuation); **OR** **(ii)** hold a degree in Property Economics or Valuation (at least level 8 in the National Framework of Qualifications); **OR** **(iii)** hold an equivalent professional qualification; (b) have, after obtaining the qualifications referred to at (a) above, at least five years satisfactory experience in negotiating property transactions, including, leasing, letting, acquisition and disposal of land and buildings, together with experience in the valuation of all real estate and in giving evidence as an expert witness in court proceedings and arbitration hearings; (c) possess a high standard of training and experience; (d) have an awareness of Health and Safety Legislation and Regulations and their application in the workplace. *Candidates are required to upload documentation for the qualification they are claiming eligibility under with their online application. The onus is on the candidate to ensure that the correct documentation is uploaded along with their application form.* *Non\-Irish Qualifications must be accompanied by a determination/comparability statement from Quality and Qualifications Ireland (QQI) to establish their comparability against the Irish National Framework of Qualifications, overseas qualifications must also be accompanied by a translation document.* ***Candidates who do not submit the required eligibility documentation by closing date/time will be deemed as having an incomplete application and no further consideration will be given to your application.*** **Duties** **The duties of the postinclude, but are not limited to, the following: \-** * Assembly (by agreement or a Compulsory Purchase process), site preparation and due diligence items and active land management measures; * Advising on and negotiating terms for the structuring of Development Agreements in respect of “Greenfield” and “Brownfield” sites, redevelopment projects, including, mixed use regeneration developments and residential development projects; * Drafting of development briefs, marketing brochures, sales particulars, tenders, etc. and liaison with external sales/letting agents and other professional advisors appointed. Participation in tender assessments; * Advising on residential property matters under various headings, including; Part V, Negotiated turn\-key acquisitions, Tenant\-Situ house purchases, Leasing, Tenant Purchase, provision of Social Housing, Homeless initiatives, appraisal of estate typologies (houses and apartments) and market analysis; * Determining capital and rental valuations for all types of property, including municipal buildings; * Providing valuation advice and negotiating terms in statutory cases, including, Compulsory Purchase and Vesting procedures, Derelict Site/Vacant Land levies/taxes and Landlord and Tenant; * Providing expert evidence at Arbitration, Valuation Tribunal and Court hearings; * Reporting on and negotiating terms in respect of the acquisition, leasing, licensing and disposal of property for all purposes; * Participation on Working and Project Groups; * Engaging on property matters with other Local or State Authorities; * Relating well to others and maintaining positive working relationships; * Such other duties as may be required from time to time. **The particular duties and responsibilities attached to the post may vary from time to time, without changing the general character of the duties or level of responsibilities entailed. The post holder may therefore be required to perform duties appropriate to the post, other than those detailed above, and to take instructions from and report to, an appropriate Officer or such designated Officer as may be assigned from time to time by the City Council.**

title: F-Gas AC Technician
description: We are currently recruiting for an **F\-Gas AC Technician** to join our Maintenance Division. This full\-time permanent position will cover the Leinster region with a particular focus on the Dublin and surrounding areas. They will be responsible for maintaining, troubleshooting and repairing air\-conditioning and refrigeration systems containing fluorinated greenhouse gases (F\-Gases). The ideal candidate will have hands\-on experience with HVAC systems, strong technical knowledge of refrigeration cycles and a valid F\-Gas qualification in accordance with EU Regulation (EC) No. 517/2014\. **PRINCIPAL ACCOUNTABILITIES** * Commission and service air\-conditioning and refrigeration systems, including split units, VRF/VRV systems and commercial chillers * Conduct leak checks in compliance with F\-Gas regulations and maintain accurate logs * Troubleshoot and diagnose system faults and perform repairs * Recover, handle and charge refrigerants safely using approved procedures * Perform routine preventive maintenance to ensure system efficiency and reliability * Maintain accurate site records including maintenance checklists, inspection reports and test results and F\-Gas registers * Advise customers on energy\-efficient system use, upgrades and compliance requirements * **Safety and Compliance:** Ensure all work complies with health \& safety standards and environmental legislation * Follow all site safety procedures and risk assessments in compliance with company health and safety policies * **Sustainability Initiatives:** Identify opportunities to improve energy efficiency, reduce waste and implement sustainable practices * Be flexible in your approach to allow for and adapt to the evolving needs of the site, client and company * Promote and uphold our core values of Care, Integrity and Excellence when engaging with clients * Be open to engaging in Continuous Professional Development (CPD) as necessary to meet the evolving needs of the role * Always behave in a manner fitting to the business ethos and maintain the Company's standards * Act as a brand ambassador as you represent the Company through its ongoing expansion * Any other duties as deemed necessary for the efficiency of the business **SKILLS AND KNOWLEDGE REQUIRED** * Valid **Category I or II F\-Gas Certification** (or equivalent recognised qualification) * Minimum of 5 years' experience working in a similar role * Knowledge of HVAC systems essential * Additional HVAC/R manufacturer training (Daikin, Mitsubishi, Panasonic, etc.) an advantage * Preferably has knowledge of low\-GWP refrigerants and evolving environmental regulations. * City \& Guilds 2079 or equivalent technical qualifications. * Must possess a valid Safe Pass and Manual Handling certificate * Full clean Drivers Licenses as travel to alternative sites will be required * Excellent people skills while delivering exceptional customer service * Proven health \& safety awareness with a thorough understanding of quality systems **WHAT WE OFFER** * Competitive salary based on experience * Company vehicle, tools and equipment will be provided * Continuous training and certification support * Opportunities for career progression within the HVAC/R field * Supportive team environment and strong safety culture

title: Fiduciary Monitoring Specialist – Funds / Depositary
description: **Fiduciary Monitoring Specialist – Funds / Depositary** Dublin \| Day Rate Contract \| Contract to May 2027 \| Hybrid 3 days per week on site We are working with a leading financial services organisation seeking an experienced **Fiduciary Monitoring Specialist** to join their Dublin\-based Funds / Depositary team on a long\-term day rate contract. This is an excellent opportunity to join a well\-established global business and gain broad exposure across fiduciary oversight, regulatory compliance and fund monitoring. **Key Responsibilities:** **Fiduciary Oversight** * Carry out investment and borrowing restriction monitoring across designated funds. * Monitor all relevant aspects of investment management activity including securities lending, OTC / exchange\-traded derivatives and third\-party held assets. * Complete cash flow monitoring and track issues through to resolution. * Support the setup of new funds across investment monitoring, NAV review, cashflow monitoring and asset register requirements. * Work closely with internal support teams and external stakeholders to resolve client issues. * Manage client interaction in respect of breaches and compliance matters. * Ensure timely and accurate information flow to clients and service providers. **Projects** * Participate in regulatory change projects, process improvements and standardisation initiatives. * Work with fiduciary teams across different jurisdictions on wider business projects. * Support additional projects and tasks as required by senior leadership. **Person Specification:** **Knowledge \& Experience** * 3\+ years’ experience in Fiduciary, Trustee, Depositary, Compliance or related financial services. * Good understanding of Trustee / Depositary standards and regulatory responsibilities. * Knowledge of UCITS and AIFMD investment restrictions would be an advantage. * Experience with exceptions\-based monitoring systems within Trustee / Depositary, custody, fund accounting or reconciliation environments would be beneficial. * Technical knowledge of financial instruments and investment strategies. **Skills** * Strong teamwork skills with a flexible and adaptable approach. * Strong knowledge of Microsoft Office, particularly Excel. * Experience with Excel tools, VBA and SQL would be highly desirable. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving ability. * Ability to manage multiple priorities while maintaining attention to detail. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills. **Qualifications** * Relevant funds industry qualifications preferred. **Competencies** * High level of integrity and strong professional ethics. * Strong team player with a collaborative approach. * Self\-starter with the ability to use initiative and manage deliverables from start to finish. * Strong attention to detail and organisational ability. * Analytical and technical mindset with strong numeracy skills. * Ability to understand derivatives within the context of regulatory frameworks. This contract offers excellent exposure across fiduciary oversight, fund administration, investment management, transfer agency, middle office and custody operations. Apply now or contact me for a confidential discussion.

Building on a century of innovation, our 48,000 associates help deliver comprehensive science\-based solutions, data\-driven insights and world\-class service to advance food safety, maintain clean and safe environments, and optimize water and energy use, and improve operational efficiencies and sustainability for customers in the food, healthcare, life sciences, hospitality and industrial markets in more than 170 countries around the world. Deliver expert service and support for water treatment and filtration systems * Monitor chemical usage and system performance to ensure optimal operation * Troubleshoot and resolve equipment and treatment issues with a focus on cost\-effective solutions * Conduct preventive maintenance and repairs to maximize equipment uptime * Maintain accurate service documentation and communicate clearly with customers * Collaborate with internal teams to continuously improve service delivery **Position Details** * This position will be based in Leinster * The territory for this position covers Ireland * Occasional overnight travel **What’s Unique About This Role** * You’ll operate independently in a dynamic field environment, supported by a team that values your expertise * You’ll work with diploma or equivalent * Position requires a current and valid Driver’s License with no restrictions * Experience in a technical service role, ideally with water filtration systems or in industrial/mechanical environments * No immigration sponsorship available for this position **Physical Requirements Of Position** * Working in confined spaces and extreme conditions (hot, cold, damp, dusty) * Lifting, kneeling, crouching, reaching, and standing for extended period *Ecolab is committed to providing reasonable accommodation to qualified individuals with a disability.* **Preferred Qualifications** * Associate’s degree in chemistry, mechanical systems, or related field * Hands\-on plumbing experience and familiarity with industrial equipment * Experience with boilers, cooling towers, and wastewater treatment systems * Knowledge of mechanical, electrical, and pumping systems * Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite **What’s In It For You** * Starting on Day 1: Access to The opportunity to take on some of the world’s most meaningful challenges, helping customers achieve clean water, safe food, and healthy environments. * The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth. * The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best. their best. title: Finance Lawyer

title: Financial Accountant
description: **Who we are** -------------- VOIS (Vodafone Intelligent Solutions) is a strategic arm of Vodafone Group Plc, creating value for customers by delivering intelligent solutions through Talent, Technology \& Transformation. As the largest shared services organisation in the global telco industry with 30,000 FTE, our portfolio of next\-generation solutions and services are designed in partnership with customers across Vodafone Group, local markets, and partner markets to simplify and drive growth. With our strategic partner Accenture, we work alongside our Vodafone customers, other Telco and tech companies to drive transformation, meet the challenges of our industry and ensure we stay relevant and resilient. This partnership is a unique, industry\-first model which brings together the best of in\-house and 3rd party capability. We work with customers across 28 countries from 10 VOIS locations: Albania, Egypt, Hungary, India, Romania, Spain, Turkey, UK, Germany, Ireland, and with a network of teams in Czech Republic, Italy, Greece, and Portugal. \#VOIS \#BeUnrivalled \#CreateTheFuture**Location** ------------ Dublin **About this role** ------------------- We are seeking a qualified Financial Accountant to support IFRS 15 financial reporting within Vodafone Ireland. This role sits within the Financial Operations team and works closely with Reporting and Finance colleagues. It offers an opportunity to strengthen technical accounting expertise while collaborating with a wide range of stakeholders and contributing to accurate and compliant financial reporting.**What you’ll do** ------------------ * Lead the delivery of monthly IFRS 15 reporting to Group, ensuring accuracy, completeness and timeliness * Support monthly Group reporting and annual statutory reporting in line with IFRS 15 requirements * Act as a key point of contact for IFRS 15\-related queries from Group Reporting * Maintain up\-to\-date process documentation and support a strong controls environment * Collaborate with the Shared Service Centre in Pune, fostering knowledge sharing and continuous improvement * Support internal audit processes, including responding to queries and strengthening controls * Assist with external statutory audits, including preparing deliverables and coordinating stakeholder inputs * Identify inefficiencies and contribute to process improvements, including automation and AI\-enabled solutions **Who you are** --------------- * A qualified accountant (CIMA, ACCA or ACA) with strong technical accounting knowledge, ideally IFRS 15 * Knowledgeable in controls, process documentation, and audit practices * Comfortable working collaboratively across teams, including offshore delivery models * Highly organised, able to prioritise and manage multiple responsibilities * Proactive in identifying and implementing process improvements using digital tools * Strong analytical capability with the ability to interpret data and apply sound judgement * A confident communicator, able to convey technical concepts clearly to diverse stakeholders **Not a perfect fit?** ---------------------- Concerned you may not meet every requirement? Vodafone is committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive. If you are excited about this role but your experience does not align exactly with every aspect of the job description, you are encouraged to apply. You may be the right candidate for this or another opportunity, and the recruitment team will support you in exploring where your skills fit best.**What's in it for you** ------------------------ * Competitive salary * Flexible working arrangements to support work\-life balance * Generous employer pension contribution * Free phone bill and mobile device discounts * Access to extensive learning and development, including Vodafone University * Exclusive discounts for family and friends on Vodafone products * 23 days annual leave, 3 charity days, plus a day off for your birthday * Free parking * Access to wellness facilities including gym and health services **What skills you will learn** ------------------------------ * Advanced IFRS 15 technical accounting and reporting expertise * End\-to\-end financial reporting and statutory compliance processes * Stakeholder management across global and offshore teams * Audit readiness and controls optimisation * Process improvement through automation and AI\-enabled tools * Data analysis and financial interpretation for decision\-making **VOIS Equal Opportunity Employer Commitment** ---------------------------------------------- Vodafone recognises and celebrates the value of diversity in building a workforce that reflects the customers and communities it serves. No form of discrimination is tolerated. This includes, but is not limited to, discrimination based on race, colour, age, veteran status, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, pregnancy, maternity or parental status, ethnicity, disability, religion or belief, political affiliation, trade union membership, nationality, citizenship, indigenous status, medical condition, HIV status, neurodiversity, social origin, cultural background, marital or civil partnership status, or socio\-economic background.**Join Us** ----------- At Vodafone, we’re working hard to build a better future. A more connected, inclusive and sustainable world. As a dynamic global community, it's our human spirit, together with technology, that empowers us to achieve this. We challenge and innovate in order to connect people, businesses, and communities across the world. Delighting our customers and earning their loyalty drive us, and we experiment, learn fast and get it done, together. With us, you can be truly be yourself and belong, share inspiration, embrace new opportunities, thrive, and make a real difference.**Alert** --------- Apply for Vodafone jobs only through the official Vodafone Careers website to avoid job scams and fraud.” \#JDEnhancedByTARA**Follow us on social media and \#StayConnected** ------------------------------------------------- * LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/vois/ * Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/voisglobal * Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/voisglobal/ * You can also chat with our employees to learn more about our projects: https://lnkd.in/dpkrcvR2

This role sits within the Financial Operations team and works closely with Reporting and Finance colleagues. It offers an opportunity to strengthen technical accounting expertise while collaborating with a wide range of stakeholders and contributing to accurate and compliant financial reporting. **What You’ll Do** * Lead the delivery of monthly IFRS 15 reporting to Group, ensuring accuracy, completeness and timeliness * Support monthly Group reporting and annual statutory reporting in line with IFRS 15 requirements * Act as a key point of contact for IFRS 15\-related queries from Group Reporting * Maintain up\-to\-date process documentation and support a strong controls environment * Collaborate with the Shared Service Centre in Pune, fostering knowledge sharing and continuous improvement * Support internal audit processes, including responding to queries and strengthening controls * Assist with external statutory audits, including preparing deliverables and coordinating stakeholder inputs * Identify inefficiencies and contribute to process improvements, including automation and AI\-enabled solutions **Who You Are** * A qualified accountant (CIMA, ACCA or ACA) with strong technical accounting knowledge, ideally IFRS 15 * Knowledgeable in controls, process documentation, and audit practices * Comfortable working collaboratively across teams, including offshore delivery models * Highly organised, able to prioritise and manage multiple responsibilities * Proactive in identifying and implementing process improvements using digital tools * Strong analytical capability with the ability to interpret data and apply sound judgement * A confident communicator, able to convey technical concepts clearly to diverse stakeholders **Not a perfect fit?** Concerned you may not meet every requirement? Vodafone is committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive. If you are excited about this role but your experience does not align exactly with every aspect of the job description, you are encouraged to apply. You may be the right candidate for this or another opportunity, and the recruitment team will support you in exploring where your skills fit best. **What's In It For You** * Competitive salary * Flexible working arrangements to support work\-life balance * Generous employer pension contribution * Free phone bill and mobile device discounts * Access to extensive learning and development, including Vodafone University * Exclusive discounts for family and friends on Vodafone products * 23 days annual leave, 3 charity days, plus a day off for your birthday * Free parking * Access to wellness facilities including gym and health services **What Skills You Will Learn** * Advanced IFRS 15 technical accounting and reporting expertise * End\-to\-end financial reporting and statutory compliance processes * Stakeholder management across global and offshore teams * Audit readiness and controls optimisation * Process improvement through automation and AI\-enabled tools * Data analysis and financial interpretation for decision\-making **VOIS Equal Opportunity Employer Commitment** Vodafone recognises and celebrates the value of diversity in building a workforce that reflects the customers and communities it serves. No form of discrimination is tolerated. This includes, but is not limited to, discrimination based on race, colour, age, veteran status, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, pregnancy, maternity or parental status, ethnicity, disability, religion or belief, political affiliation, trade union membership, nationality, citizenship, indigenous status, medical condition, HIV status, neurodiversity, social origin, cultural background, marital or civil partnership status, or socio\-economic background. **Join Us** At Vodafone, we’re working hard to build a better future. A more connected, inclusive and sustainable world. As a dynamic global community, it's our human spirit, together with technology, that empowers us to achieve this. We challenge and innovate in order to connect people, businesses, and communities across the world. Delighting our customers and earning their loyalty drive us, and we experiment, learn fast and get it done, together. With us, you can be truly be yourself and belong, share inspiration, embrace new opportunities, thrive, and make a real difference. **Alert** Apply for Vodafone jobs only through the official Vodafone Careers website to avoid job scams and fraud.” \#JDEnhancedByTARA **Follow us on social media and \#StayConnected** * LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/vois/ * Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/voisglobal * Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/voisglobal/ * You can also chat with our employees to learn more about our projects: https://lnkd.in/dpkrcvR2

title: Financial Crimes Compliance Analyst
description: The Role will report directly to the Head to Anti\-Financial Crime, Ireland and serves as a subject matter expert (SME) playing a critical role in safeguarding our European businesses against financial crime risks. Sitting within the second line Anti‑Financial Crime (AFC) function, this role carries significant responsibility in supporting Vanguard's Financial Advisor Services (FAS) business in the execution of the Financial Crime framework (which consists of AML and Terrorist financing, Sanctions, Bribery and Corruption and Tax Evasion) during the distribution of Vanguard UCITS range and oversight of Irish Fund's Anti\-Money Laundering (AML) and Counter\-Terrorist Financing (CFT) programme. The Role will report directly to the Head to Anti\-Financial Crime, Ireland and serves as a subject matter expert (SME) playing a critical role in safeguarding our European businesses against financial crime risks. Sitting within the second line Anti‑Financial Crime (AFC) function, this role carries significant responsibility in supporting Vanguard's Financial Advisor Services (FAS) business in the execution of the Financial Crime framework (which consists of AML and Terrorist financing, Sanctions, Bribery and Corruption and Tax Evasion) during the distribution of Vanguard UCITS range and oversight of Irish Fund's Anti\-Money Laundering (AML) and Counter\-Terrorist Financing (CFT) programme. **Core Responsibilities** * Support the maintenance and enhancement of the European AFC Framework, including methodology updates, documentation improvements, and alignment to group‑wide standards. * Advise the FAS business in respect to compliance with the Vanguard global financial crime policy requirements including local European obligations. * Create, update, and manage the Anti Financial Crime policies, procedures, and supplements. * In collaboration with the UK AFC team execute an assurance program for the areas that Vanguard Europe insources and support the creation of specific financial crime testing programs for oversight of outsourced third\-party relationships. * Review and analyse escalations in respect to due diligence, screening and transaction monitoring alerts. Resolves exceptions, including unusual and complex issues escalated by more junior team members, according to established guidelines. Collaborates with managers to resolve the most complex issues. * In collaboration with the UK AFC team execute of the Vanguard Europe annual risk assessments methodology for AML, Sanctions and Bribery and Corruption including detailed data gathering, quantitative and qualitative analysis, drafting of risk narratives, and coordination of validation and challenge with stakeholders across the First and Second line of defence. * Support the submission of periodic regulatory and internal AFC reporting for Vanguard Europe and the FAS business, ensuring all submissions are accurate, timely, complete, and delivered according to defined governance and regulatory expectations. * Assist project governance by helping prepare papers, progress updates, risk logs, and milestone reporting. * Provide formal and informal educational activities, including presentations, staff meetings and training programs. Develops and delivers financial crimes related training and education for Vanguard Europe crew members. * Maintains a comprehensive knowledge of the investment industry and compliance developments with implications for Vanguard's financial crimes program. Keeps abreast of the current market, regulatory and industry news. Serve as a consultant on compliance related issues. Acts as a liaison to regulatory agencies.Strong understanding of Anti Money Laundering/Counter Terrorist financing, sanctions and Anti Bribery and Corruption requirements, risk, regulatory expectations, industry practice, business systems and processes in Ireland and the UK. **Essential** **Skills \& Experience** * Significant practical experience delivering or coordinating risk assessments, including data analysis, narrative writing, and validation stages. * Demonstrated experience managing regulatory or internal reporting cycles, including drafting, coordinating, and quality‑assuring submissions. * Experience working with policy governance, supporting policy refresh cycles, or maintaining standards frameworks. * Strong communication skills, both written and verbal, with experience drafting governance papers, risk assessments, or policy content. * High attention to detail, strong organisational skills, and ability to work under pressure in a fast‑paced environment. * Ability to interpret regulatory expectations and apply them pragmatically. **Desirable** * Experience operating across multiple European jurisdictions and/or global AFC structures. * Experience of supporting the distribution of an Irish UCITS range. * Exposure to transformation or change programmes (e.g., technology enhancements, operating model changes). **Qualifications:** * Minimum 5\+ years’ experience in AML/CFT, compliance, audit, or risk within financial services. Management Company/Asset Management 2nd Line AFC experience preferred. * Professional qualification relating to Financial Crime / Undergraduate degree or equivalent combination of training and experience **Special Factors:** * This position follows a hybrid working model that will require you to be in our Dublin office three days per week. * Vanguard is not offering visa sponsorship for this position. **How We Work** Vanguard has implemented a hybrid working model for the majority of our crew members, designed to capture the benefits of enhanced flexibility while enabling in\-person learning, collaboration, and connection. We believe our mission\-driven and highly collaborative culture is a critical enabler to support long\-term client outcomes and enrich the employee experience.

title: Financial Planning Advisor
description: **Fairstone Ireland** is seeking applications from experienced Financial Planning Advisors for full\-time permanent opportunities nationwide. As a Financial Planning Advisor, you’ll provide expert, independent financial advice to a diverse client base, helping them achieve their financial goals through tailored solutions. This position involves managing an established client portfolio while also focusing on growing new business opportunities. You will have significant autonomy and the chance to shape financial plans that drive measurable results. This role is part of the Fitness Probity Regime and Individual Accountability Framework, which set out the specific requirements applicable. **Responsibilities** * Deliver professional financial advice that complies with the Central Bank of Ireland’s regulations and Fairstone’s best practices. * Manage and grow an existing portfolio of clients, ensuring a proactive and client\-focused service. * Conduct thorough research and analysis to recommend financial solutions that address a wide range of client needs. * Drive new business growth through referrals, self\-generated leads, and existing Fairstone client relationships. * Ensure all client records are maintained accurately, documenting objectives, goals, and recommendations. * Stay ahead of industry trends, regulatory changes, and market developments to provide informed advice. * Actively participate in business development initiatives, contributing to the growth of client Assets Under Management (AUM) and overall revenue. **The ideal candidate** * A minimum of 12 months' experience as a Financial Planning Advisor, with a deep knowledge of financial planning, including protection, pensions, investments, tax\-efficient strategies, and holistic planning. * A proven track record of managing and growing client portfolios. * A high level of commercial awareness and the ability to identify opportunities for business growth. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build trust and explain complex information clearly and concisely. * Strong organisational skills, with a focus on accuracy and attention to detail. * A client\-centric approach, prioritising strong, long\-term relationships and exceptional service delivery. **Qualifications** * Fully QFA qualified is an absolute requirement. CFP is a distinct advantage. * In\-depth knowledge of financial regulations, markets, and industry practices. * Strong leadership qualities and the ability to mentor junior advisors, if required. * Proficient in using financial planning software and tools. Please note that any offer of employment with Fairstone Ireland is subject to the satisfactory completion of pre\-employment checks. These checks include verification of your references, education and professional qualifications, confirmation of eligibility to work in the Republic of Ireland, and a review of publicly available professional/personal information (internet search). Employment will not commence until all checks have been completed to our satisfaction. For more information, please email **hr@fairstone.ie** and the HR team will be able to assist you further.

title: Financial Reporting, Off
description: ****Who We Are Looking For:**** Based in the Dublin office, you will be part of a team of 200\+ dedicated financial reporting professionals who are focused on delivering interim and annual US GAAP/IFRS financial statements to a diverse group of alternative investment managers. ****Why This Role Is Important To Us:**** The team you will be joining is a part of State Street Global Services (SSGS). SSGS gives asset owners and managers access to the essential financial tools they need to deliver effective investment solutions. From core custody, accounting, fund administration and shareholder recordkeeping, to complete operations solutions and servicing for alternative assets like OTC derivatives, private equity and real estate, SSGS’ tools help our clients make better investment choices and act on growth opportunities. Join us if making your mark in the financial services industry from day one is a challenge you are up for. ****What You Will Be Responsible For:**** As a Financial Reporting Officer you will Plan, schedule, and manage the production of interim / annual financial statements and daily workflow for multiple alternative investment client relationships and product types (hedge, private equity, and real estate funds). * Act as the primary point of contact for clients with responsibility for implementing and supporting the service requirements of the various client relationships serviced within the group. * Perform technical reviews of hedge, private equity, and real estate funds’ financial statements in accordance with relevant GAAP and regulatory requirements. * Set individual and team targets and priorities; maintain high standards of client service; conduct performance appraisals and give feedback on individual and team performance. * Be a source of technical support and guidance, including reviewing financial statements prepared by your teams and ensuring that all financial statements are accurate and prepared in accordance with the relevant GAAP prior to issuance to external parties (clients / auditors). * Support new business integration and ensure operating models are designed to deliver best of breed service to clients leveraging global footprint as necessary. * Manage your own continued professional development, through engaging in the performance review process and attending relevant training as per agreed training curriculum. * Deputise for your assistant vice president as required from time to time. * Perform other duties as required from time to time. ****What We Value**** These skills will help you succeed in this role * Excellent communicate skills * Strong leadership abilities. * Excellent attention to detail * Strong knowledge of accounting standards as they relate to funds (IFRS, Irish GAAP in particular) * Ability to multi\-task and be comfortable with managing difficult situations as they arise. * Be able to communicate effectively with colleagues, clients, auditors at all levels. * Experience of working to deadlines. * Ability to delegate work to others. * Ability to effectively represent the team to clients and internal stakeholders ****Education \& Preferred Qualifications**** * Possess a degree in finance, business / accounting or related discipline, * Have 6\-8 years’ experience working in a similar environment or related environment, including experience managing staff * The role would also suit someone who is a qualified professional accountant (eg. ACA, ACCA, CPA). * The candidate should possess strong knowledge of US GAAP/IFRS investment company financial statements with previous experience servicing alternative investments.. **Disposition** * Self\-motivated * Good initiative * Professional * Multi\-tasking ability * Enthusiastic * Adaptable – ability to develop new approaches to solving problems * Flexible * Strong inter\-personal skills ****About State Street**** **What we do.** State Street is one of the largest custodian banks, asset managers and asset intelligence companies in the world. From technology to product innovation, we’re making our mark on the financial services industry. For more than two centuries, we’ve been helping our clients safeguard and steward the investments of millions of people. We provide investment servicing, data \& analytics, investment research \& trading and investment management to institutional clients. **Work, Live and Grow.** We make all efforts to create a great work environment. Our benefits packages are competitive and comprehensive. Details vary by location, but you may expect generous medical care, insurance and savings plans, among other perks. You’ll have access to flexible Work Programs to help you match your needs. And our wealth of development programs and educational support will help you reach your full potential. **Inclusion, Diversity and Social Responsibility.** We truly believe our employees’ diverse backgrounds, experiences and perspectives are a powerful contributor to creating an inclusive environment where everyone can thrive and reach their maximum potential while adding value to both our organization and our clients. We warmly welcome candidates of diverse origin, background, ability, age, sexual orientation, gender identity and personality. Another fundamental value at State Street is active engagement with our communities around the world, both as a partner and a leader. You will have tools to help balance your professional and personal life, paid volunteer days, matching gift programs and access to employee networks that help you stay connected to what matters to you. State Street is an equal opportunity and affirmative action employer. ****About State Street**** Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success. We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work\-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law. Discover more information on jobs at StateStreet.com/careers Read our CEO Statement Job ID: R\-788886

title: Finishing Foreperson
description: At Glenveagh, we're proud to build homes and support communities. And we build strong teams that enable us to do that. As one of Ireland's Best Large Workplaces in 2025, and the first construction company in Ireland to be accredited with the Gold Investors in Diversity mark, we create a workplace where everyone feels supported, and able to grow their career. We're looking for people who want to play their part in delivering homes to individuals and families, and all while availing of great benefits and the opportunity for long term career development. We are currently recruiting a Finishing Foreperson for our residential project in Mooretown, North County Dublin. **Responsibilities:** * Liaise and communicate closely with the design team, Site Manager and other stakeholders * Coordinate the daily resourcing and control of site labour and ordering of materials, completing the online daily site diary and relevant weekly documentation in accordance with company policy * Provide key point of contact for purchasers * Liaise closely with Sales * Coordinate snagging crews, overseeing effective completion of snag lists * Carry out company compliance and administration procedures and recording performance and progress of site operations and subcontractors * Effective planning of tasks and efficiently organising the plant and site facilities in order to meet deadlines * Ensure works are carried out in compliance with building regulations, health and safety and environmental standards and to a high quality **Requirements** * Degree / Higher Diploma qualification in Construction Management and/ or equivalent trade qualification * Significant experience in a similar position * Excellent knowledge of trades, and in particular carpentry * Background in residential construction * Excellent working knowledge of latest building regulation and certification standards * An ability to achieve demanding time and quality targets **Benefits** * Company vehicle * Competitive salary in one of Ireland's fastest growing PLCs * Comprehensive health insurance for you and your dependents * Contributory pension scheme * An exciting Wellness Programme with events and activities running throughout the year * Access to our Digital Gym * Employee Assistance Programme * Performance related bonus * Regular compensation reviews * Long term illness cover * Peace of mind with life assurance * Paid maternity leave, as well as paternity leave for fathers * If you're cycling, we've got you covered on the cycle\- to\- work scheme Glenveagh is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from suitably qualified candidates from all sections of the community. If you require reasonable accommodation in completing this application, interviewing, or otherwise participating in the recruitment process, please notify the Talent Acquisition team by contacting careers@glenveagh.ie.

title: Fire and Security Technician in the Leinster region
description: We are a nationwide fire and security company with active projects in some of the largest, exciting developments in Ireland. Such as renewable energy projects, large state of the art warehousing projects and large housing complexes. Due to expansion, we are currently recruiting a position which will offer an exciting opportunity to an enthusiastic candidate. The right candidate will find this role varied, interesting and rewarding. This role would suit somebody with a advance knowledge of security systems, past experience essential. The position will provide great experience working with several types of systems on various projects in the Leinster area. This is a great opportunity to commence a long term career in an exciting modern and growing company. Salary will reflect entry level and experience. Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €45,000\.00\-€60,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Company car * Company pension * Flexitime * Food allowance * Sick pay Work Location: On the road

title: Food & Beverage Assistant
description: As a **Food \& Beverage Assistant,** you'll be part of a dynamic, fast\-paced team, contributing to a positive and friendly work environment. The role offers numerous opportunities for growth within a company that values individuality and invests in its employees. Your hard work will be recognised and rewarded, making it an excellent chance to advance in a supportive setting. **Your Key Responsibilities Will Include** * Prepare and serve a range of food and beverages, including barista\-style coffees and drinks * Deliver excellent customer service, ensuring a positive experience for all customers * Set up service counters and maintain attractive food and drink displays * Process customer payments accurately and efficiently * Ensure all service and work areas are clean, tidy, and well\-organised at all times * Follow food hygiene and Health \& Safety procedures in line with training * Operate and maintain coffee machines and other catering equipment safely, reporting any issues * Ensure proper storage of food items, ingredients, and equipment after service * Restock supplies, including crockery, ingredients, and beverages as required * Work collaboratively with team members and support colleagues across all areas * Respond to customer requests with a positive attitude and strive to exceed expectations **Our Ideal Food \& Beverage Assistant Will** * Bring a positive, can\-do attitude to everything you do * Show genuine passion for delivering exceptional customer service * Excel as a supportive and collaborative team player * Embrace the excitement of thriving under pressure * Demonstrate impeccable time management and reliability * Prioritise safety in every task you undertake * Previous experience in a similar catering role is a bonus, but your enthusiasm is what truly matters At Compass Ireland, our people are our business. When you join the Compass Group you will be joining a supportive network of incredible individuals, each united by their passion for going the extra mile for the organisations we partner with. We are building a team who care about providing a great customer experience and want to grow and develop as we do. We are dedicated to investing in every colleague joining us and pride ourselves on being an equal opportunity employer; we celebrate diversity and are committed to building an inclusive environment for all our colleagues. We consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Please review our Privacy Notice for details on how we maintain, protect and why your personal data is collected when you apply for a job with Compass Group. We will not retain any CV's or job applications for longer than necessary. Job Reference: com/1806/98711001/52815187/ROI \#Republic of Ireland Compass Group UK\&I is committed to fostering an environment where every individual can truly be themselves at work and has equal opportunities to advance in their careers. We strive to build a culture that respects and celebrates the unique talents, beliefs, backgrounds, and abilities of all our team members. We want our colleagues to feel valued, empowered to reach their full potential, and to thrive \- because diversity is our strength!

title: Full Stack Developer
description: **Company Description** Version 1 has celebrated 30 years in business and continues to be trusted by global brands to deliver technology and transformation solutions that drive customer success. Our deep expertise enables our customers to navigate the rapidly evolving technology landscape. We foster strong partnerships with global technology leaders including Microsoft, AWS, Oracle, Red Hat, OutSystems, Snowflake, ensuring that our customers are provided with the highest quality solutions and services. We’re an award\-winning employer reflecting how our employees are at the very heart of what we do: * UK \& Ireland's premier AWS, Microsoft \& Oracle partner * 3300\+ strong, €350/£300m revenue business * 10\+ years as a Great Place to Work in Ireland \& UK * Best Workplace for Women in the UK \& Ireland by GPTW * Best Workplace for Wellbeing in the UK by GPTW We’re a core values driven company, we hire people who share our values, and we reward those who display and foster them, it’s deeply embedded within our DNA. Invest in us and we’ll invest in you!. **Job Description** We are seeking a Full Stack Developer to join our team. The successful candidate should have experience across backend and frontend development, including modern JavaScript frameworks and Microsoft technologies. This role involves working on both new and existing systems, contributing to development, testing, and deployment activities. We are looking for someone with analytical problem\-solving skills, attention to detail, and the ability to collaborate effectively within a team environment while communicating technical concepts clearly. **Key Responsibilities** * Design, build, implement, test, and monitor high\-quality software components for full stack applications * Develop and maintain backend services and APIs * Contribute to frontend development using modern frameworks * Write unit tests to support code quality and reliability * Support CI/CD pipelines for build, deployment, and testing processes * Participate in Agile development practices, including sprint planning, stand\-ups, and retrospectives * Contribute to code reviews and shared coding standards **Qualifications** **Essential Skills \& Experience** * Experience as a Full Stack Developer * Backend development using **.NET Core (C\#)** and **NodeJS/Typescript** * Frontend development using **Angular** or **React**, with **TypeScript** * Experience with **SQL Server** and/or **PostgreSQL** * Understanding of **REST API design and integration** * Experience with **DevOps tools** (e.g. Azure DevOps, GitHub Actions, or similar) * Experience supporting **application deployment and CI/CD processes** * Experience developing **scalable features and services** * Problem\-solving skills with a structured and thoughtful approach * Understanding of software development principles and best practices * Experience writing **unit tests** and participating in **code reviews** * Ability to collaborate within cross\-functional teams and communicate technical concepts clearly **Desirable Skills** * Knowledge of **SSIS** and ETL workflows, or interest in developing these skills * Experience with **data migration** activities or exposure to related tools and processes * Experience with **Power BI** and data platforms such as SQL Server or Oracle * Experience working with **AWS services** (e.g. S3, Lambda, Cognito, Amplify) * Experience working with **SSO and SAML** * Experience building products using **LLMs** * Basic prompt engineering knowledge or interest in learning * Interest in developing new systems and projects **Additional Information** At Version 1, we believe in providing our employees with a comprehensive benefits package that prioritises their wellbeing, professional growth, and financial stability. * Salary Range: €45,000\-€60,500 * Share in our success with our Quarterly Performance\-Related Profit Share Scheme, where employees collectively benefit from a share of our company's profits * Strong Career Progression \& mentorship coaching through our Strength in Balance \& Leadership schemes with a dedicated quarterly Pathways Career Development programme * Flexible/remote working, Version 1 is tremendously understanding of life events and people’s individual circumstances and offer flexibility to help achieve a healthy work life balance * Financial Wellbeing initiatives including; Version 1 Pension with a 5% employee\-matched contribution, Private Healthcare Cover, Life Assurance, Financial advice and an Employee Discount scheme * Employee Wellbeing schemes including Gym Discounts, Bike to Work, Fitness classes, Mindfulness Workshops, Employee Assistance Programme and much more. * 24 days holiday allowance plus Public Holidays, enhanced maternity/paternity leave, marriage/civil partnership leave and special leave policies * Educational assistance, incentivised certifications, and accreditations, including AWS, Microsoft, Oracle, and Red Hat * Reward schemes including Version 1’s Annual Excellence Awards \& quarterly Star Awards. * Environment, Social and Community First initiatives allow you to get involved in local fundraising and development opportunities as part of fostering our diversity, inclusion and belonging schemes. And many more exciting benefits… drop us a note to find out more. Version 1 is an equal opportunities employer We are committed to building a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace where everyone feels valued and able to thrive. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities and lived experiences, and we value the different perspectives people bring including those shaped by disability and neurodiversity. We want every candidate to have a positive and accessible recruitment experience. If you need reasonable adjustments at any stage of the process, please contact the recruiter at ekta.bahl@version1\.com. We will consider all requests carefully, respectfully and confidentially. \#LI\-EB1 \#LI\-Hybrid

title: Full Stack Software Engineer, Charles River Development, Officer
description: **Who we are:** Charles River Development (CRD) is the FinTech division of State Street, of which Charles River Private Market’s (CRPM) offers holistic solutions across clients public and private portfolios. Our vision is to be the world’s leading investment platform, shaping the future of the industry and driving new levels of technology\-led innovation, resiliency, efficiency and growth for investors and the people they serve. CRD Engineering organization is innovating and transforming the platform by moving away from monolithic software to utilising next generation cloud\-based technology that leverages Microsoft Azure, Kafka, Snowflake, etc. Industry momentum has seen CRD grow over 135% in headcount over the last 5 years, and we continue to grow. As a digital innovation leader, we invest into our solutions, processes, systems, and talent. We are actively hiring engineers for our Private Market’s division as this is an exciting time to join our innovative engineering teams as we are investing in growing out our European presence and leadership to help drive our next phase of growth. **Summary:** Act as Full Stack engineer responsible for the development, enhancement and maintenance of the CRPM modules and components, as part of an agile scrum team, and at times provide troubleshooting assistance to customer support teams. **Responsibilities:** * Analyze, design, develop, test, and debug large and complex software enhancements and solutions critical to system operation * Collaborate with engineering team to create simple and sustainable software solutions for complex problems * Direct problem solving for projects or major phases of projects to resolve software technical issues * Provide informed guidance and critical analysis of proposed changes during code reviews * Write unit and automation tests to ensure a high\-quality product * Provide troubleshooting on large, mission critical client implementations * Engage in the agile software development process by adhering to and advancing the CRD scrum methodology, including attending all daily standups, sprint planning, backlog grooming, and retrospectives * Plan and coordinate cross\-team activities to complete assignments **Qualifications:** **Recommended Education:** * B.S. degree (or foreign education equivalent) in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, and Physics or other technical course of study required. MS degree strongly preferred. **Key skills and prior experience:** * Experience of 3\-5 years developing event driven architectures, financial services a plus * Experience in cloud native development using Java, Spring, Angular or React * Experience in developing observable, operable cloud native software that horizontally scales * Experience with relational databases (RDBMS) * Demonstrated problem solving abilities involving multi\-tiered, high\-performance database applications * Demonstrated ability to design and develop software solutions to business and technical problems of high complexity * Outstanding written and verbal communication skills * Able to work well with peers in a collaborative team environment * Knowledge of Agile development methodology * Employees are expected to work from the office 4 days a week. **About State Street** **What we do.** State Street is one of the largest custodian banks, asset managers and asset intelligence companies in the world. From technology to product innovation we're making our mark on the financial services industry. For more than two centuries, we've been helping our clients safeguard and steward the investments of millions of people. We provide investment servicing, data \& analytics, investments research \& trading and investment management to institutional clients. **Work, Live and Grow.** We make all efforts to create a great work environment. Our benefits packages are competitive and comprehensive. Details vary in locations, but you may expect generous medical care, insurance and savings plans among other perks. You'll have access to flexible Work Program to help match your needs. And our wealth of development programs and educational support will help you reach your full potential. **Inclusion, Diversity, and Social Responsibility.** We truly believe our employees' diverse backgrounds, experience and perspective are a powerful contributor to creating an inclusive environment where everyone can thrive and reach their maximum potential while adding value to both our organization and our clients. We warmly welcome the candidates of diverse origin, background, ability, age, sexual orientation, gender identity and personality. Another fundamental value at State Street is active engagement with our communities around the world, both as a partner and a leader. You will have tools to help balance your professional and personal life, paid volunteer days, matching gift program and access to employee networks that help you stay connected to what matters to you. State Street is an equal opportunity and affirmative action employer. Company: Charles River Development Discover more at www.StateStreet.com/careers **About State Street** ====================== Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success. We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work\-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law. Discover more information on jobs at StateStreet.com/careers Read our CEO Statement

title: Full Transparency - Here's What Happens After You Apply
description: * **Full transparency: from our hiring process to our promotion structure** * **Full meritocracy: 100% merit\-based promotions \+ uncapped commission** * **We value your time: interview with us and receive feedback within 48 hours** **Access** is looking for driven individuals to join us as **Recruitment Consultants** \- no prior experience needed, full training provided \- as we push forward on our mission to become Ireland's \#1 healthcare recruitment agency. We're open to all backgrounds. It's 2026\. Candidates use AI to write CVs. Recruiters use AI to review them. Both ghost each other. We've all been there, and we're done with it. At Access, we do things differently. **Here's exactly what happens after you hit apply:** **The Process:** 1. **You Apply \-** Submit your CV. If there's a fit, you'll hear from me directly: no AI screening, no black hole. I'll give you a quick call to complement what's on your CV and get a feel for your story. If it's a match, your profile goes straight to our hiring managers. 2. **Interview Stage 1 \-** A conversation about your background, your understanding of the role, and your motivation. We're assessing relevant skills and how you think about recruitment. 3. **Interview Stage 2 \-** A short roleplay, live feedback, then another go. We're not grading perfection. We're grading coachability, and grit. 4. **Interview Stage 3 \-** A final chat with one of our directors. We're looking for alignment with Access's values: ownership, accountability, and ambition. 5. **After the Final Stage \-** We move fast. You'll have an answer within 24 hours. Accept the offer, sign the contract, and we get to work. ***Guaranteed: feedback within 48 hours at every interview stage.*** **Compensation:** * Competitive base salary \+ unlimited commission * **Year 1 average total earnings: €45K\+ \| Year 2 average total earnings: €75K\+** * Commission paid on every shift you book: no clawbacks, no limits * Top performers consistently earn well above these figures from day one **What We Look For** * 1\-3 years of proven success in any background (commercial, target\-driven, and fast\-paced environments are very beneficial) * Ambitious, coachable, accountable, and hungry to grow fast * EU citizenship or Stamp 4 visa Ready to start a process you can actually trust? **\+353 (1\) 572 1161 \| cesar.toscano@accesshealthcare.ie**

title: Fundraising Ambassador - MSF
description: **Looking for more than just a job?** Want to earn well, build confidence, and make a real difference every day? Join **Médecins Sans Frontières (Doctors Without Borders)** as a **Face‑to‑Face Fundraising Ambassador**, representing one of the world’s most trusted humanitarian organisations. You’ll be out in local neighbourhoods, having real conversations, sharing MSF’s life‑saving work, and inspiring people to support medical care where it’s needed most. **This role is NATIONWIDE:** **PLEASE NOTE:** A **full driving licence and access to your own vehicle are essential for this role.** No experience? **No problem.** Full training is provided from day one. **What you’ll be doing:** * Engaging with members of the public in residential areas * Representing MSF’s mission with passion and professionalism * Signing supporters up to long‑term, life‑saving donations * Working as part of a supportive, high‑energy outdoor team **Who this role is perfect for:** * Confident, friendly communicators who enjoy meeting people * Those happy working outdoors and on their feet * Reliable, motivated, and goal‑driven individuals * Caring, community‑minded people who want meaningful work * **Drivers with a full licence and access to a vehicle** **What you’ll get in return:** * **Base salary:** €2,166\.67 – €2,250\.00 **per month (gross)** * **Uncapped commission** – your effort directly increases your earnings * **Fully paid, industry‑leading training \& onboarding** with major Irish charities * Clear progression opportunities, including **leadership \& self‑development training** * **Opportunity to take Fridays off** when weekly targets are reached * Supportive management, coaching, and a strong team culture **Your Working Week:** * **Monday – Friday** * **Variable hours depending on site and campaign** * **Summer Shifts \- 11am \- 7pm OR 12pm \- 8pm** **1‑hour lunch break included**
